items.title: Karl-Anthony Towns has not scored a point in the 4th quarter in the NBA Finals, even as he's had a very good series for the Knicks.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Karl-Anthony Towns has not scored a point in the 4th quarter in the NBA Finals, even as he's had a very good series for the Knicks.

His combined 4th quarter stats against the Spurs:

29.1 minutes played
0-of-6 from the field
0-of-2 on 3s
0 FT attempts

items.title: Three Things I Noticed in Knicks-Spurs Game 3
items.author: Last Night In Basketball
items.body_excerpt: Hit the jump for the Game 3 edition of Three Things, starring the Spurs' defense, Stephon Castle, and OG Anunoby.

items.title: Shooting in the last 7 seconds of the shot clock in the Finals...
items.author: John Schuhmann
items.body_excerpt:
Shooting in the last 7 seconds of the shot clock in the Finals...

Knicks: 29-for-93 (31%), 13-fo-40 (33%) from 3.

Spurs: 11-for-35 (31%),  6-for-19 (32%) from 3.

The Knicks' 93 FGA in the last 7 seconds are more than they had in the conf. semis (68) or conf. finals (90).

items.title: Castle when told that Wemby said he "might be the most mature player on the team":
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Castle when told that Wemby said he "might be the most mature player on the team":

"Honestly, probably, yeah, other than Vic. Probably Vic would be number one. I don't go to sleep at 9 p.m., so ... I'm probably a close second."

items.title: The Los Angeles Angels are 6-for-20 in save opportunities, as a team.
items.author: Blake Murphy
items.body_excerpt:
The Los Angeles Angels are 6-for-20 in save opportunities, as a team.

The 2024 White Sox went 21-for-58 (36.2%), which is the all-time futility rate.

Finally, the Angels could get in the recordbook somehow.

items.title: Total Net Points | Game Three
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Total Net Points | Game Three

Victor Wembanyama and Steph Castle help power the Spurs to victory in a must win game three.

images.alt:
Alt text:

Horizontal bar chart titled “Knicks vs Spurs | Total Net Points | NBA Finals | Game Three.” Orange bars represent Knicks players; black bars represent Spurs players. Bars extend right for positive Net Points and left for negative.

Top positive performers: Victor Wembanyama leads all players at +7.5, followed by Stephon Castle +4.1, OG Anunoby +3.4, Julian Champagnie +2.8, KAT +2.2, Josh Hart +1.9, Jordan Clarkson +1.3, Mitchell Robinson +1.2, Luke Kornet +1.1, Devin Vassell +0.8, and Jeremy Sochan +0.3.

Negative performers: Jose Alvarado -0.7, Carter Bryant -0.9, Keldon Johnson -1.6, Brunson -1.6, Miles McBride -2.1, Mikal Bridges -3.5, De’Aaron Fox -4.5, Dylan Harper -5.3, and Landry Shamet -6.3.

X-axis runs from -12 to +12. Data source: ESPN Analytics. Visualization by @HoopInformatics.bsky.social.
